quote_text: If you will take care of this faculty and consider it your only possession, you will never be hindered.
reflection: Which faculty of judgment needs your care before the day gets noisy?

quote_text: No man has ever been so far advanced by Fortune that she did not threaten him as greatly as she had previously indulged him.
reflection: What good fortune can you hold without depending on it?

quote_text: To act against one another, then, is contrary to nature.
reflection: Where would conflict soften if you remembered you were made for cooperation?

quote_text: He is free who lives as he wishes to live; who is neither subject to compulsion nor to hindrance, nor to force.
reflection: What dependence can you release to practice real independence?

quote_text: We should not believe the lack of silver and gold to be proof of the simple life.
reflection: Where can simplicity be honest rather than performative?

quote_text: One thing, and only one, philosophy.
reflection: What single principle can conduct you through a scattered day?

quote_text: Well, what if it does happen? Let us see who wins!
reflection: Which feared outcome can you meet by asking what your character would do?

quote_text: No man is bad without suffering some loss and damage.
reflection: How can seeing another person's damage help you answer more justly?

quote_text: Our life should observe a happy medium between the ways of a sage and the ways of the world at large.
reflection: Where can moderation make your discipline more livable?

quote_text: Nothing is so damaging to good character as the habit of lounging at the games.
reflection: What entertainment leaves your character worse than it found it?

quote_text: The man who has seen his own blood, who has felt his teeth rattle beneath his opponent's fist, who has been downed in body but not in spirit.
reflection: What hard contact with reality can train your courage?

quote_text: For another will not damage you, unless you choose.
reflection: Where can you refuse to let another person's conduct damage your own?

quote_text: Eat merely to relieve your hunger; drink merely to quench your thirst.
reflection: What need can you meet plainly without turning it into excess?

quote_text: The chief business is this: distinguish and separate things.
reflection: How will you separate what is yours from what only appears urgent?

quote_text: Virtue will be as efficacious as excessive fear.
reflection: Where can virtue move you more strongly than fear?

quote_text: Those who require proofs at every point, even where the matter is perfectly clear, are completely inept and dull-witted.
reflection: Where is justice already clear enough to act on?

quote_text: It is the sign of an unstable mind not to be able to endure riches.
reflection: How can you use comfort without letting comfort use you?

quote_text: For truth has its own definite boundaries.
reflection: Which rumor or assumption needs the boundary of truth?

quote_text: You must pass sleepless nights, endure toil, go away from your kinsmen, be despised by a slave.
reflection: What cost of progress are you willing to count before beginning?

quote_text: The nature of women no less than men to be pleased by good and just acts and to reject the opposite of these.
reflection: How can you recognize moral strength where habit might overlook it?

quote_text: He removes all desire from himself, and he transfers aversion to those things only of the things within our power which are contrary to nature.
reflection: What desire or aversion needs to be moved back inside your power?

quote_text: How quickly all things disappear, in the universe the bodies themselves, but in time the remembrance of them.
reflection: What becomes clearer when you remember how quickly things pass?

quote_text: You will suffer soon enough, when it arrives; so look forward meanwhile to better things.
reflection: What suffering can you stop rehearsing before it arrives?

quote_text: When friendship is settled, you must trust; before friendship is formed, you must pass judgment.
reflection: Where does loyalty require either wiser judgment or fuller trust?

quote_text: If you would improve, submit to be considered without sense and foolish with respect to externals.
reflection: What appearance are you willing to lose for real self-command?

quote_text: The man who is goaded ahead by hope of anything, though it be within reach, is troubled and unsure of himself.
reflection: Which hope is making your mind less steady than it could be?

quote_text: When you have decided that a thing ought to be done and are doing it, never avoid being seen doing it.
reflection: What right action deserves visibility despite criticism?

quote_text: No good thing is pleasant to possess, without friends to share it.
reflection: What good can become more just by being shared?

quote_text: Hold fast to it and establish it firmly, in order that what is now impulse may become a habit of the mind.
reflection: What good impulse needs repetition until it becomes character?

quote_text: We do not put to the test those things which cause our fear; we do not examine into them.
reflection: What fear needs examination before obedience?

quote_text: For whatever of these things results, it is in my power to derive benefit from it.
reflection: What unwelcome result can still become training?
